subject.title: Psychology
subject.description:
IB Psychology is a Group 3 subject in the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP). The course examines human behavior and mental processes through scientific inquiry, exploring biological, cognitive, and sociocultural approaches to understanding behavior. Students develop critical thinking skills while investigating contemporary psychological research, research methods, and ethical considerations in psychological practice.

The course is available at Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours) and Higher Level (HL, 240 hours), with first assessment in 2027.

Assessment consists of Paper 1 (integration of concepts, content, and contexts through short-answer and extended-response questions: SL 2 hours, HL 2 hours, SL 50%, HL 40%), Paper 2 (application of psychological knowledge to research contexts: SL 1 hour, HL 2 hours, SL 25%, HL 20%), Paper 3 (HL only: research methods and data analysis, 1 hour, 20%), and Internal Assessment (research proposal development: SL 25%, HL 20%).

strengths: Clear and operationalised aim with explicit real-world relevance
Accurate description of the dual-processing model and its link to the investigation
Fully operationalised IV and DV with precise hypothesis and alpha level
weaknesses: Lacks discussion of competing heuristics or alternative decision-making models
Hypothesis notation could be improved by formally labelling Hâ
Relevance explanation, though concrete, is somewhat brief
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands of the Introduction reach the 5â6 band, with two strands solidly at 5 and one at 6. Minor omissions (alternative theories, hypothesis notation, slight brevity in relevance) prevent a top-of-band 6 overall, so a score of 5 best reflects the quality and balances the minor gaps.

strengths: Design is clearly explained with a rationale for its choice
Sampling technique is justified for reducing demand characteristics
Controlled variables and materials are not only listed but their purposes are explained
weaknesses: Sampling description was truncated in placesâfull assignment procedure could be clearer
Minimal discussion of how age control affects reliance on System 1
No consideration of alternative materials (e.g., digital vs printed)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three Exploration strands consistently meet the 3â4 band descriptors at the top level. The design, sampling and materials are not only described but also explained, justifying the maximum of 4 for this criterion.

strengths: Appropriate choice and application of descriptive (medians, IQR) and inferential (Mann-Whitney U) statistics
Clear statement linking non-significant U value to failure to reject the hypothesis
Box-and-whisker plot addresses the hypothesis by contrasting conditions
weaknesses: Graphical presentation lacks axis labels/units and excludes outliers without justification
No effect size reported to quantify the magnitude of the difference
Discussion of dispersion is noted but missing numerical measures of variability
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While statistical analysis and interpretation meet the 5â6 band, the weaknesses in graphical presentation (missing labels, excluded outliers) and omission of effect size limit the work to the 3â4 band. A mid-band score of 3 reflects solid statistical technique but flawed presentation.

strengths: Strengths and limitations are clearly linked to the investigation and explained
Modifications are explicitly tied to limitations with justifications
Findings discussion references the original study and model
weaknesses: Discussion of findings is brief and lacks deeper theoretical exploration
Generalizability claim is overstated given the sample
Suggested methodological modifications lack detail on implementation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands (strengths/limitations, modifications) clearly reach the 5â6 band but the findings discussion is only at the 3â4 level and is superficial. Balancing these, an overall score of 3 (mid 3â4 band) appropriately reflects the mix of high and limited performance.

strandSelections.reasoning: A box-and-whisker plot is included and explicitly addresses the hypothesis by contrasting effort conditions. However, axis labels/units are minimal and some outliers were deliberately removed, which constitutes a graphical error. These shortcomings place the work in the 3-4 band; the mid-band score of 3 reflects partial but imperfect execution.

The student clearly states that the U value is non-significant, links this to failure to reject the null, and interprets what this means for the effort heuristic. Interpretation is tied back to data (medians) and hypothesis. While concise, the linkage is accurate and meets the 5-6 criteria; slight brevity merits a 5 rather than 6.

Both IV (high vs low stated production times with precise hour/day values) and DV (estimated monetary value in SEK) are clearly stated and operationalised. The directional research hypothesis and null hypothesis with alpha level are provided. The clarity and full operational detail meet the top descriptor, warranting the full 6 marks.

Opportunity sampling is named, participant characteristics (age 15-17, no prior psychology) are given, and the choice is justified as reducing demand characteristics. This moves beyond description into explanation, matching the higher band for this strand. Coverage is thorough enough for the upper end of 3-4.

The dual-processing model and the effort heuristic are both accurately described, with key features (System 1/2, cognitive misers) correctly outlined. The student then explains how the heuristic underpins the present investigation and why System 1 conditions were induced, demonstrating an explicit connection between theory and procedure. Some discussion of competing theories is absent, so a solid 5 rather than 6 is awarded.

Findings are discussed in light of the original Kruger et al. study and linked to possible use of System 2 thinking, showing integration with background theory. The discussion, however, is brief and lacks deeper theoretical exploration, fitting solidly within the 3-4 range at the upper point.

The student states a clear aim â to investigate whether greater perceived production time leads to higher monetary valuations (effort heuristic) â and then explicitly explains why this question matters for real-world advertising practice. The relevance is therefore more than merely identified; it is explained in concrete commercial terms, satisfying the 5-6 descriptor. Minor brevity in the real-world link justifies a mid-band, not the very top.

Three strengths and three limitations are each directly related to design or procedure and are explained, not just listed. Links to validity (demand characteristics, variability) are explicit, satisfying the 5-6 descriptor. Minor depth gaps keep the score to 5 rather than 6.

Five controlled variables are listed with clear justifications, and each material (presentation, stopwatch, questionnaires) is linked to its purpose. The student therefore explains, rather than merely lists, both controls and materials, fulfilling the 3-4 descriptor at the higher point.

Each proposed modification (niche objects, matched-pairs design, price range) is clearly tied to a stated limitation and justification is given. The linkage and justification meet the highest-band criteria; slightly limited methodological detail prevents a full 6, leading to a 5.

The design (independent measures) is not only identified but accompanied by a rationale aimed at reducing demand characteristics. Procedure steps show how each condition was administered, thus explaining, not merely describing, the design. This aligns with the 3-4 band; the explanation is adequate and coherent, so the top of the band (4) is appropriate.

Appropriate descriptive (median, IQR) and inferential (Mann-Whitney U) statistics are selected for ordinal, independent-groups data and are applied without evident calculation errors. Significance is reported with U, z and p. Minor omissions (e.g., effect size) prevent a perfect 6, but accuracy and appropriateness justify a 5.

annotations.comment: References draw on seminal and recent sources, demonstrating breadth in background research.
Reference formatting is inconsistent (e.g., missing italics, dates, access information); apply a uniform citation style (APA) throughout.
Include discussion of competing heuristics or alternative decision-making models to deepen the theoretical framing and demonstrate awareness of contrasting perspectives.
Clarify the operational choice to measure quality through monetary value, explaining why price estimation captures perceived object quality effectively.
The independent variable is clearly operationalized with specific productionâtime values across objects, ensuring precise manipulation of effort levels.
The dependent variable is explicitly defined and measured in SEK, making the outcome quantifiable and directly linked to the research question.
The student effectively links the investigationâs relevance to real-world advertising, showing why dramatizing product creation time can influence perceived monetary value.
The description of the dual-processing model and the effort heuristic is clear and accurate, with appropriate reference to System 1âs reliance on cognitive misers.
The citation âCharlotte C.â lacks detail; provide a full reference (author, year, source) to ensure traceability of the design description.
Materials are described with clear links to inducing System 1 thinking, enhancing reproducibility and validity of the procedure.
The one-tailed hypothesis is directional and operational, clearly predicting higher valuations for longer stated production times.
Consider labeling the null hypothesis formally as Hâ and matching notation with the alternative to improve clarity in hypothesis presentation.
The independent measures design is well explained with rationale to reduce demand characteristics, showing understanding of experimental control.
Expand on how controlling participant age ensures equivalent cognitive maturity and influences reliance on System 1 processing.
Including irrelevant contextual details to mask the aim is an effective control against demand characteristics, showing thoughtful procedural design.
The sampling description is cut off here; ensure the complete account of how participants were selected and assigned.
Consider whether printed answer sheets versus a digital interface could affect response speed or accuracy and discuss potential impact.
The stepwise procedure is detailed and logically ordered, promoting clear replication of the experiment.
The discussion of dispersion identifies high variability but omits reporting effect size or variance coefficients to quantify spread.
Findings are well stated, linking the non-significant U-value to failure to reject the hypothesis and contextualizing the data.
Stating demand-characteristics mitigation and time constraints as strengths is effective, but the claim of global generalizability from this sample is overstated.
Justification for excluding participants with prior psychology experience is strong, reducing bias from prior knowledge.
The table of medians and IQRs correctly applies descriptive statistics for ordinal data, aiding clear comparison across conditions.
Add axis labels and units to the box-and-whisker plot to enhance interpretability and ensure clarity.
Appropriate use of the Mann-Whitney U test for ordinal independent samples demonstrates correct inferential choice.
Report an effect size (e.g., r) alongside the p-value to provide a measure of the magnitude of the difference.
The discussion proposes System 2 thinking as an explanation; also consider alternative influences such as cognitive load or task complexity.
Limitations are clearly linked to design and procedure, demonstrating reflection on validity and sampling influences.
Proposed matched-pairs design needs detail on matching criteria and procedures to ensure equivalence and minimize variability.
The conclusion succinctly summarizes outcomes and acknowledges methodological factors influencing results.
Expand the conclusion to discuss implications for theory development and outline directions for future research beyond commercial application.
